payload.summary: The amendment identifies a real dedupe collision bug. The model change (lines 469-482) now permits distinct named runs to share a started_at timestamp, but BeadStarted events (service.rs:448-451) only include project_id in details. Since the journal deduplicates on (timestamp, event_type, bead_id, details), two starts at the same timestamp become indistinguishable and the second is silently dropped. Completion events already include run_id/plan_hash via completion_journal_details() — start events should do the same.

votes.rationale: Verified in code: completion_journal_details() (model.rs:333) already includes run_id and plan_hash, but record_bead_start (service.rs:448-451) only writes `project={project_id}`. The fs.rs dedupe key (timestamp, event_type, bead_id, details) cannot distinguish two BeadStarted events from different named runs sharing the same started_at. This is a genuine correctness bug introduced by the model relaxation at lines 469-482 that allows new named runs to proceed despite a prior finalized run at the same timestamp. The fix — widening BeadStarted details to include run identity — is consistent with how completion events already work and is necessary to maintain journal audit trail integrity.
